121812.explanation: 'Must' is too absolute. The availability of candidates determines the aim for an equal number of men and women. Many executive boards have an odd number of aldermen.

121816.explanation: The municipality has neither the funds nor the capacity to deploy enforcement officers and the like 24 hours a day.

121817.explanation: Our municipality arranges this through the installation of a heating network and geothermal heating.

121819.explanation: Our municipality is in solidarity with other municipalities that take in refugees. Moreover, we comply with the distribution law.

121823.explanation: Wasting 20,000 euros in fifteen minutes is a waste of money.

121824.explanation: This prevents illegal dumping in public spaces.

121826.explanation: The municipality cannot pay for everything for everyone. Budgets will soon be in the red.

121833.explanation: We have supported a motion of this nature.

121834.explanation: Mayor and aldermen are too cautious here, while there is more need.

121836.explanation: This has not been discussed within the party.

121838.explanation: In certain neighborhoods, our residents suffer from tenants from, for example, Dordrecht.

121843.explanation: If it can be agreed upon well, then yes. Strongly encourage mentally and physically healthy people to contribute to a beautiful municipality through work.

121844.explanation: Our municipality has a scheme for this that provides for it to a limited extent.

121845.explanation: This has already been done in our poverty policy. A limit of up to 130 percent of the minimum.

121848.explanation: Our party has called for more 30 km/h zones with a motion.

121851.explanation: Given the personnel problem, this is not feasible. Moreover, religion should not influence public services.

121853.explanation: A municipality cannot pay for everything for everyone when it comes to normal living expenses.

121855.explanation: Does not make a significant difference for us.

121862.explanation: Build somewhat higher, but no apartment blocks of 10 or more residential floors.

121863.explanation: This causes nuisance; especially from labor migrants.

121864.explanation: Good for social cohesion. It can prevent loneliness and lead to early detection of problems. Care can be close to the people.

121865.explanation: The garages already cost us a lot of money.

121866.explanation: The parking garages are adequate. Moreover, the first 2 hours are free (paid for by the companies and shops).

121812.explanation: This shows that women and men are equal. The executive board benefits from this.

121816.explanation: Within a certain margin, that should be possible.

121817.explanation: We do not focus on this because we support the installation of the heating network. Geothermal energy is also a possibility that we preferably want to offer collectively.

121819.explanation: We are critical of asylum reception and believe that residents, entrepreneurs, and shopkeepers should be involved in the location choice. But at the same time, there is a distribution law that the municipality must comply with. The municipality cannot pretend that the law does not apply to it.

121823.explanation: The municipality of Papendrecht organizes an annual fireworks show together with the municipality of Dordrecht to minimize nuisance and still have a joint event that as many residents as possible can enjoy.

121824.explanation: Collecting bulky waste does not have to be free as long as it remains affordable. Fortunately, the collection of bulky waste in Papendrecht is well arranged and not too expensive.

121825.explanation: In Papendrecht, we hardly experience festivals and parties where noise standards are greatly exceeded, except possibly for the annual fair which only lasts a few days. The existing noise standards are sufficient.

121826.explanation: The municipality can engage in dialogue with the entrepreneur and jointly consider what can be done. Ultimately, it is the entrepreneur’s responsibility, but the municipality could financially support in the interest of the general public.

121828.explanation: The local broadcaster receives funding from a separate fund for broadcasters; in Papendrecht the regional broadcaster is active. Where necessary, the municipality can indirectly and occasionally support to elevate independent journalism to a higher level.

121832.explanation: Monuments should be maintained and, in principle, a municipality should not cut back on this.

121833.explanation: We agree with that. In fact, we have advocated through a motion to expand the number of enforcement officers (BOAs). We would like to increase the number of BOAs from 7 to 10.

121834.explanation: We are not in favor of camera surveillance because we consider privacy in public spaces very important. In specific cases, we can make exceptions with justified reasons.

121838.explanation: We agree with that, because housing shortages also exist among native residents, especially regarding affordable housing.

121841.explanation: If it contributes to solving the problem, then yes. If the zoning allows it, the municipality does not necessarily have to co-finance plans from project developers that operate commercially competitive.

121843.explanation: An understandable thought and certainly worth trying where possible.

121844.explanation: The fact that the municipality offers that option and that it is also used (in a legally valid way) indicates that it is a facility that is needed.

121845.explanation: National legislation is leading here; the municipality follows laws and regulations in executing social domain policies. The municipality already spends a lot of money on the social domain. People who work and earn too little must be helped.

121848.explanation: If it concerns national legislation, the municipality must comply, but on certain routes a 50 km/h limit is necessary to enforce, also for traffic flow and emergency services. In the city center and on the dike, 30 km/h has already been partially implemented.

121850.explanation: At this moment, a 30 km/h zone already applies in several places in the center. An environmental zone takes a step further. Polluting vehicles will need to be replaced by less polluting vehicles (electrification) in the coming years.

121851.explanation: In principle yes if there is a choice, but otherwise no. The important thing is that people who need care actually receive that care. We therefore nuance this statement on the word 'always'.

121853.explanation: That does not belong to the primary tasks of the municipality but to everyone's personal responsibilities.

121855.explanation: The tourist tax does not have to increase, except for correction due to inflation and price index. We must keep Papendrecht as attractive as possible for tourists and business overnight stays.

121862.explanation: If we have to build additional affordable housing, we cannot avoid building upwards in some places. We are not fundamentally opposed to that, provided that (high) new construction fits the surroundings.

121863.explanation: That is basically up to the landlords themselves. Renting out single rooms can be a solution for people who must be in Papendrecht regularly but for short periods, for example due to caregiving or work. But we want to prevent abuse, so no illegal (sub)letting.

121864.explanation: On Papendrecht scale this is very ambitious (in every neighborhood), but we do support expanding the number of community centers. The goal of each neighborhood having its own community house/social space is a good idea. It is important that there is support for this.

121865.explanation: Currently, it is not very expensive to park in the center. Residents in the center should be able to (continue to) get a permit through a regulated system so that parking their car on public roads remains affordable.

121866.explanation: As parking pressure increases, expansion of parking spaces must be considered. The expansion of GKN Fokker has made the parking problem in the center worse. Parking at transferia on the outskirts of the municipality is a better solution.

121868.explanation: The municipality actively campaigns for diversity policy, including the Orange the World campaign (helping to eliminate violence against women and girls worldwide). It is important to continue paying attention to this. The municipality also has a rainbow path.

121812.explanation: As VVD, we are a liberal party that emphasizes freedom, responsibility, and equal opportunities for individuals, regardless of gender. The right person in the right place.

121813.explanation: The VVD considers engaged citizens important but does not see referenda as the right instrument for decision-making. In our view, residents are better involved through consultation, participation processes, and discussions with the municipal council.

121816.explanation: The VVD believes that entrepreneurs should have as much freedom as possible. Cafes and restaurants should in principle be able to determine their own opening hours, with room for municipalities to provide tailored solutions where livability and safety require it.

121817.explanation: The VVD thinks residents should be able to decide on sustainable energy themselves, and that municipalities should mainly facilitate and simplify rules. Financial support can come from national funds.

121819.explanation: Papendrecht already contributes to the shelter of Ukrainians and unaccompanied minor asylum seekers (AMVs). We want clear choices: no extension of current locations, authority returned to the council. The Hague must provide structural solutions; the dispersal law puts too much pressure on municipalities.

121823.explanation: The Papendrecht VVD sees municipal fireworks displays as a safe and festive alternative to fireworks if a nationwide ban on private fireworks is imposed. Good organization and permits are paramount so residents can enjoy without nuisance.

121824.explanation: The Papendrecht VVD believes the collection of bulky waste should be free of charge so residents do not incur extra costs and illegal dumping is prevented.

121825.explanation: The Papendrecht VVD does not want stricter noise standards for events. We find it important to maintain a good balance between livability and organizing festivities and want events to be able to continue under existing rules.

121826.explanation: We support an accessible society but not additional municipal subsidies or obligations for entrepreneurs. Entrepreneurs should retain freedom, costs should remain low, and rules should not grow further.

121828.explanation: The Papendrecht VVD is cautious about additional subsidies. It must first be clear that the local broadcaster works efficiently, remains independent, and delivers demonstrable added value. Extra funds only temporarily and purposefully, not structurally.

121832.explanation: The Papendrecht VVD opposes generic cuts to monument care. As a party, we want to preserve heritage but efficiently, with more private involvement and less subsidies where possible.

121833.explanation: The VVD in Papendrecht agrees with strengthening safety and enforcement. The party supports measures for better deployment of enforcement officers (BOAs) and more attention to safe neighborhoods.

121834.explanation: The Papendrecht VVD is in favor of more camera surveillance where needed for increased safety.

121836.explanation: The Papendrecht VVD agrees: BOAs who supervise safety may be equipped with batons to better protect themselves and residents.

121838.explanation: We understand the need for local priority but believe everyone should be treated equally. This includes people with economic ties to Papendrecht.

121841.explanation: As Papendrecht VVD, we disagree with this statement. We believe the market should determine the space and municipalities should not impose obligations to convert vacant offices into housing with subsidies.

121843.explanation: The VVD in Papendrecht believes welfare recipients should do socially appropriate work in exchange for their benefits to encourage involvement and strengthen society.

121844.explanation: The Papendrecht VVD thinks waiving municipal taxes for residents with low incomes should end. Everyone should contribute fairly to local costs so the municipality remains financially healthy.

121845.explanation: As Papendrecht VVD, we believe residents should bear as much responsibility as possible themselves. We are cautious about structurally raising the income threshold for poverty assistance unless this is temporarily necessary to effectively support people.

121848.explanation: As the Papendrecht VVD, we oppose a general reduction to 30 km/h. We believe that traffic safety is better achieved through enforcement and smart measures, not through rigid speed reductions that unnecessarily slow down traffic.

121850.explanation: As the Papendrecht VVD, we oppose an environmental zone. It unnecessarily hinders residents and entrepreneurs and harms the local economy.

121851.explanation: The Papendrecht VVD believes that patients should have freedom of choice in healthcare and that care should fit their needs. However, there is no explicit policy that a care provider can always be chosen based on religion, culture, or belief.

121853.explanation: We find it important that everyone can make ends meet, but we do not consider a compulsory scheme for free tampons and sanitary pads through the municipality necessary.

121855.explanation: As the Papendrecht VVD, we oppose an increase in the tourist tax. We believe this places extra pressure on entrepreneurs and visitors and prefer a stable and predictable tax climate in our municipality.

121862.explanation: The Papendrecht VVD is against even more high residential towers. Many flats are already being built or are in development. We advocate for a balanced mix of buildings so that everyone has a suitable housing choice. Projects in development should be realized as soon as possible.

121863.explanation: The VVD wants more space for landlords and fewer rules to make renting out housing easier. We are positive about room rental and temporary rental as long as this increases the housing supply and municipalities are not unnecessarily restricted.

121864.explanation: In Papendrecht, the VVD chooses tailored neighborhood solutions instead of a compulsory community center for every neighborhood. We focus on cooperation with residents and local initiatives to improve livability in each neighborhood.

121865.explanation: We agree with this: cheap or free parking downtown attracts visitors and supports entrepreneurs. As in Papendrecht’s parking garages, where the first 2 hours are free. This keeps our center accessible and attractive.

121866.explanation: The VVD believes there is sufficient parking available in and around the center. Additional parking spaces are not necessary; the space can be better used for greenery, hospitality, and meeting places.

121868.explanation: The Papendrecht VVD values diversity but opposes mandatory annual campaigns. We prefer to give room to local initiatives and cooperation so that diversity is promoted effectively and practically.

121812.explanation: Equal representation should always be the goal. Unfortunately, this will not always be possible. In such cases, it is necessary that it is as equal as possible.

121813.explanation: Papendrecht residents have the option to request a referendum. We will not take that away from our residents. The gap between residents and the town hall must be bridged, and abolishing participation opportunities contradicts this.

121816.explanation: Within reasonable limits, every entrepreneur should be able to determine their own opening hours. In case of nuisance (for example, disturbing night rest), the municipality must be able to intervene and impose opening hours.

121817.explanation: Residents who, for example, unite in an energy cooperative help the energy transition and often relieve the energy grid. The municipality should support that, including financially where necessary.

121819.explanation: Papendrecht takes responsibility and contributes where necessary to solving the shelter shortage, within what is socially realistic and acceptable. When this is no longer needed, it can be considered whether it can be closed.

121823.explanation: The fireworks show has been a great success in recent years. Many residents enjoy it. With the upcoming fireworks ban, our fireworks show is a nice way to jointly welcome the new year.

121824.explanation: Processing waste inevitably costs money. It is therefore logical that offering waste also costs money. However, it must remain reasonable. By having the municipality also pay part of the costs itself, we keep a close eye on the total costs.

121825.explanation: The center of Papendrecht could be much livelier. That means, among other things, more space for events.

121826.explanation: It is very important that buildings are accessible to every Papendrecht resident, but it is up to the entrepreneur to realize that. The municipality can support this, but the entrepreneur must also contribute. The municipality is responsible for an accessible public space.

121828.explanation: The local broadcaster is an important source of Papendrecht news and entertainment. Local media should be valued by the municipality.

121832.explanation: There have already been enormous budget cuts over the past decades. The few monuments Papendrecht has, including the church clock of the Grote Kerk on the dike, must be cherished and protected.

121833.explanation: Safety cannot only be improved through repression, but especially by placing more emphasis on prevention. However, the priorities of the enforcement officers (boa's) must be better aligned with the needs of residents.

121834.explanation: Camera surveillance is always symptom treatment and not a solution to the actual problem. Moreover, it only shifts the problem outside the view of the cameras.

121836.explanation: Our enforcement officers (boa's) have received good training to resolve problems with words. If it escalates, the police are the appropriate authority to act. We should not want to put our boa's in such situations.

121838.explanation: Those who want to continue living in Papendrecht should be able to. Mainly singles who leave a family home should get priority. This way, we want a suitable, affordable home to become available for every Papendrecht resident.

121841.explanation: The housing shortage forces us to be innovative with available space. Converting vacant buildings into sustainable, affordable housing is a good option for this.

121843.explanation: Nobody wants to be on welfare for fun. We help people to get back to work with guidance, not with coercion. We try to connect sectors facing staff shortages with this group.

121844.explanation: Anyone can find themselves in a situation where bills cannot be paid temporarily. The municipality can provide peace of mind by granting debt relief and thus preventing larger debt problems.

121845.explanation: There is an increasing group of working poor. These are people who work full time but still cannot make ends meet. Support must also be available for this group.

121848.explanation: Where it is safely possible, we must reduce speed. This is safer, which means fewer deaths and injuries in Papendrecht. In addition, it is cleaner and actually ensures smoother traffic flow. 50 km/h on main roads will be the exception.

121850.explanation: The air quality in Papendrecht is very poor due to its location between two busy highways. However, Papendrecht’s center is not suitable for implementing an environmental zone and the effect will likely be limited. We choose to improve air pollution throughout Papendrecht, for example by tackling cut-through traffic.

121851.explanation: Attention to personal background, wishes, and beliefs is very important for valuable care. We strive to be able to provide everyone with such care.

121853.explanation: We will offer free hygiene products at the town hall and library. In addition, we encourage schools and other organizations to do the same.

121855.explanation: The tourist tax in Papendrecht is relatively low, also compared to surrounding municipalities. By raising it to the regional average, we increase our income without deterring visitors.

121862.explanation: Space in Papendrecht is scarce. To still build the necessary homes without sacrificing parks and greenery, we must see where we can appropriately build upwards. This must fit the environment and the social cohesion of a neighborhood. We will do this sustainably with, for example, green walls.

121863.explanation: To counter the housing shortage in the short term, individual room rentals must remain an option. In the long term, we must build enough to minimize the necessity of room rentals. However, the rent must be affordable and not intended for quick large profits.

121864.explanation: Community centers increase social cohesion in a neighborhood and are a good means against loneliness and alienation. A cup of coffee, a listening ear, and a fun activity work wonders. We applaud existing initiatives.

121865.explanation: The parking garages at the Meent currently offer the first 2 hours free. This does not need to be extended. We prefer to encourage people to come to the center by public transport, bike, or on foot.

121866.explanation: There is sufficient parking in the garages at the Meent. We prefer to realize more (guarded) bicycle parking to make it more attractive to come to the Meent by bike.

121868.explanation: Papendrechters come in all shapes and sizes. We must appreciate that diversity and there should be attention for it. This way, we show in words and actions that everyone is a Papendrechter and belongs here. We exclude no one.

121812.explanation: The CDA finds it important that men and women, people with disabilities, youth and elderly from every neighborhood are represented in our municipality. However, we will not mandate this by law. In practice, we remain dependent on suitable and available candidates.

121813.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of a referendum. The municipal council is elected to make difficult trade-offs. The CDA does find it important to involve citizens in other ways on important issues.

121816.explanation: The CDA does not consider it desirable to leave opening hours unrestricted for various reasons, one of which is respect for the nighttime rest of our residents.

121817.explanation: The CDA supports energy cooperatives and local residents who take initiatives to make the environment more sustainable. The municipality can support this but should not be a co-financer. Not every form of sustainable energy fits in Papendrecht (for example, wind turbines do not).

121819.explanation: The CDA supports the council with respect to respecting the existing national distribution law, whereby Papendrecht contributes proportionally to the demand for asylum reception, just like all other municipalities.

121823.explanation: The CDA wants to keep the possibility of fireworks on New Year's Eve, even after a private fireworks ban. We are convinced that in the longer term a joint fireworks show with surrounding municipalities will prove to be a good and positive alternative after December 31, 2026.

121824.explanation: The CDA favors the possibility of doorstep pickup of bulky waste by HVC, but it does not have to be free. Waste processing costs money. We pay waste disposal fees for that. The CDA wants to keep fees as low as possible. Residents who dispose of bulky waste themselves also incur costs.

121825.explanation: The CDA wants to reduce regulatory pressure in society. The municipality makes a careful consideration when granting a permit for festivals. Good enforcement, especially of closing times, is also important.

121826.explanation: The municipality has a task to make public spaces accessible. The CDA allocates money for that. Making and keeping business buildings accessible is the responsibility of the businesses themselves.

121828.explanation: The local broadcaster receives a contribution from the municipality. Nationally, a proposal is coming for extra funding to local broadcasters from the central government. There will be a system change: local broadcasters will disappear and be replaced by regional broadcasters.

121832.explanation: The CDA believes we must be careful with our limited number of monuments. Of course, costs will always remain a concern.

121833.explanation: The CDA supports more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs). Possibly, this can help improve the safety feeling of our residents. Such a decision, in our opinion, should only be taken after developing a joint clear vision on the matter.

121834.explanation: The CDA wants to increase safety in our municipality. Extra camera surveillance can help detect crime more effectively and quickly. It greatly assists the police in tackling crime.

121836.explanation: The CDA thinks it would be sensible if things align with, for example, national or at least regional policies.

121838.explanation: We want to give priority to people with a local connection so that young people can continue to live in their own municipality. You only have a local connection if you have lived in Papendrecht for six years. The CDA has ensured that every municipality can include this priority in the housing ordinance.

121841.explanation: The CDA believes that 'vacant offices' should not automatically be converted into homes. For example, if there are enough better alternatives for housing, conversion need not happen. Not every vacant office building is automatically suitable as a residential location.

121843.explanation: As CDA, we certainly encourage this. But not everyone receiving social assistance is capable of doing socially meaningful work, although the CDA does encourage it.

121844.explanation: The municipality must show a humane face: waiving municipal taxes is one of the most effective measures a municipality can take to support people with a low income.

121845.explanation: The CDA finds it unacceptable that the municipality you live in determines how well you can make ends meet. Raising the threshold means helping fewer people. As a municipality, we also should not unnecessarily deviate from national rules. Help on managing a small budget can help.

121848.explanation: The CDA agrees with this, except for the main roads/neighborhood collector streets where 50 km/h remains allowed. According to the CDA, enforcing the maximum 50 km/h by speed cameras at certain locations where excessive speeding is common is part of this!

121850.explanation: Papendrecht is not a large city. The CDA expects that the costs required to implement such a measure in Papendrecht will by far not outweigh the small environmental gain.

121851.explanation: The CDA finds choice options in healthcare important. Sometimes the difference in rates is so great or the quality is so low that this is undesirable. Mental health care is available by choice and also for psychological help after a divorce or parenting support. We object to the word 'always'.

121853.explanation: Although the CDA can sympathize somewhat with this measure, we are not in favor of a generic measure in this regard. Perhaps this can be organized low-threshold via schools and food banks for those who otherwise cannot afford it.

121855.explanation: The CDA supports that the municipality of Papendrecht adjusts the current tourist tax rate to the national average price level. We do not have that many tourists in Papendrecht. A more attractive center, nightlife, theater, etc. will increase tourism.

121862.explanation: The CDA supports taller residential flats along the Veerweg-Rembrandtlaan axes, as is already current policy. This gives Papendrecht the possibility to grow slightly and also house its own residents.

121863.explanation: The CDA is against this due to purchase protection. This is a measure to protect affordable homes from investors. This way more people can buy a home themselves and there is less price inflation.

121864.explanation: The CDA agrees with encouraging neighborhood centers in the districts. Various neighborhoods have neighborhood centers, but there are also neighborhoods where there is no need. In further housing development, more neighborhood centers will automatically arise and we support that. Two neighborhoods with a neighborhood center is also fine.

121865.explanation: The CDA believes that the (limited) paid parking in Papendrecht does not need to become cheaper. Enforcing the free first 2 hours must, however, remain.

121866.explanation: The CDA believes there is little to no space for more parking spaces in and around the center. The further development of the center should still be carefully considered in this respect.

121868.explanation: The CDA feels involved in diversity and wants to make a positive contribution. Whether this is best done as an annual campaign is possible but should first be mutually coordinated.

121812.explanation: These positions are about quality. We do, however, like to see both men and women represented.

121813.explanation: The council is elected during the municipal elections. We want to improve contact with residents through a youth council, advisory council, and periodic consultations with churches and associations.

121816.explanation: We support quiet periods at night between 2:00 AM and 7:00 AM and on Sunday mornings during church time from 9:30 AM to 12:00 PM.

121817.explanation: We focus on better insulation, sustainable energy, and cooperation between residents. People in vulnerable and aging neighborhoods receive help to better insulate their homes, thereby reducing energy costs.

121819.explanation: As a municipality, due to the distribution law, we have responsibility for the reception of asylum seekers in the Netherlands. Within the limits of what is possible, help and shelter will be provided to asylum seekers.

121823.explanation: Meeting and connection are important as a society. A fireworks show can contribute to that.

121824.explanation: What can be reused, we prefer to offer first via the thrift store. The remaining bulky waste can be collected free of charge three times a year, which prevents illegal dumping.

121825.explanation: Noise standards should be phased: during the day and early evening the norm may be higher than after 11:00 PM.

121826.explanation: Businesses have their own responsibility to be accessible to people with disabilities.

121828.explanation: There will be no expansion of existing subsidies.

121832.explanation: We are happy with the existing monuments. We want to preserve them for the future.

121833.explanation: We want a safe Papendrecht. Expanding the number of BOA officers is necessary to increase their presence during evening events. The deployment of BOAs should focus more on nuisance and enforcement of rules.

121834.explanation: We want to use camera surveillance only when there is recurring vandalism or for social safety reasons in isolated areas.

121836.explanation: The role of BOAs is to detect, address, and de-escalate.

121838.explanation: People from our own municipality take priority over other home seekers. The national quota requires every municipality to accommodate status holders. In addition to our own residents, we are responsible for offering them housing. In urgent cases, we adhere to regional agreements.

121841.explanation: This is not an issue in our municipality.

121843.explanation: We consider the participation principle important so that people are part of society. With tailor-made solutions. If someone cannot participate (temporarily) due to circumstances, then we offer exceptions. We want newcomers with language deficits to start working on language development from the first year.

121844.explanation: It is a form of income support. Without debt remission, income would fall below the minimum subsistence level established annually by the Nibud. We want to prevent more financial problems and have an poverty coordinator who quickly and effectively helps people resolve debts.

121845.explanation: In our municipality, this is already set at 130 percent of the social assistance standard.

121848.explanation: Except for through connecting roads, a maximum speed of 30 km/h is a good plan for the safety of pedestrians and cyclists.

121850.explanation: Our municipality is small. We focus on the Stomp principle, which also positively contributes to the environment.

121851.explanation: Identity-based care is important for residents. Recognition of their values, norms, and religious beliefs builds trust in the aid received.

121853.explanation: People can obtain this material via the food bank.

121855.explanation: The tourist tax aligns with the national average.

121862.explanation: The height of flats under construction at intersections and away from low-rise buildings can be higher. Within more built-up areas with low-rise surroundings, we do not want high flats.

121863.explanation: Due to the housing shortage, this could be a temporary measure. With compliance to rules: no exploitation (exorbitant rents), registration in the Basic Personal Records Database, and house rules. We also offer opportunities for splitting a house (upper and lower).

121864.explanation: We are a village and distances are not large. We find it important that residents connect with each other. This requires not only community centers. Meeting places in parks and unifying activities like Neighborhood Day are important.

121865.explanation: The first two hours are free, and the hourly rate is lower than the average compared to other municipalities.

121866.explanation: We encourage people to move around the village on foot or by bike. We want more space to safely park bicycles.

121868.explanation: We think it is important that this subject receives attention in schools. It is addressed nationally and in the media.

121812.explanation: Diversity is important, but when choosing aldermen, quality must be the main criterion. The number of good candidates is often limited. Therefore, parties cannot always be required to have as many men as women.

121813.explanation: We want to increase the power of the voter by introducing a binding corrective referendum. This means that a municipal council decision can be reversed through a referendum. We will impose strict conditions on this.

121816.explanation: Entrepreneurs can determine for themselves when they are open. However, it must be ensured that there is no nuisance for neighbors.

121817.explanation: The municipality ensures residents can access all subsidies from Europe and the national government. This helps residents jointly generate sustainable energy.

121819.explanation: Papendrecht takes responsibility for people who have fled. We provide shelter with attention to the human dimension.

121823.explanation: D66 initiated the fireworks show on the river. This way, we celebrate New Year's Eve safely and festively together.

121824.explanation: Not everyone can bring bulky waste themselves. Elderly people, those with disabilities, or without a car have difficulties. If the municipality collects bulky waste for free, it helps these people. This keeps the neighborhood tidy and reduces waste on the streets.

121825.explanation: The noise standards are already very strict. If they become even stricter, almost no parties can be held anymore (except silent discos).

121826.explanation: Entrepreneurs are already required to make their buildings accessible and must pay for it themselves. The municipality can help by thinking along and providing advice, but not by giving money.

121828.explanation: Good journalism is important, also locally. Therefore, we believe the municipality should provide extra support to the local broadcaster.

121832.explanation: Papendrecht has a limited number and variety of monuments; we need to take good care of them.

121833.explanation: Papendrecht has room for 6 full-time BOAs, but there are still 2 vacancies. With 6 BOAs, that is enough. First, the open positions need to be filled; additional BOAs are (not yet) needed.

121834.explanation: We do not want extra cameras everywhere. But if there is frequent vandalism or damage, such as at the Rainbow Bridge, shelter locations, or the Fokker headquarters, we should be able to install cameras.

121836.explanation: BOAs currently do not need batons. If they themselves indicate this is important for their safety, it can be considered. At the moment, this necessity does not exist.

121838.explanation: This seems sympathetic, but if all municipalities do this, social tenants can no longer find housing outside their own municipality.

121841.explanation: Empty offices can be converted into homes. This way we use buildings better and solve the housing shortage. The municipality should assist with subsidies.

121843.explanation: Those who can work should use their time to find paid employment. That helps them get out of social assistance.

121844.explanation: People with low incomes really need this help. Otherwise, they fall into debt more quickly.

121845.explanation: If we raise the threshold, many more people will receive benefits. However, it must remain worthwhile to work.

121848.explanation: On through roads, 50 km/h should remain possible, especially for emergency services. On all other roads, 30 km/h is fine.

121850.explanation: This issue does not arise in Papendrecht. The Market Square is already low-traffic.

121851.explanation: It leads to increased costs in healthcare if everyone wants their own type of care provider.

121853.explanation: As an education municipality, we need to arrange this. Some girls miss school because they can't afford menstrual products and therefore stay home. The municipality can solve this by offering free products through schools.

121855.explanation: Tourists use facilities in our municipality: roads, parking lots, public spaces. It is fair that they contribute to that. With the extra income, the municipality can invest in the facilities everyone uses.

121862.explanation: We have little space in Papendrecht, so we must build upwards if we want more homes. But only where possible.

121863.explanation: Splitting houses should become easier. This is one of the measures that can relieve the housing shortage.

121864.explanation: Papendrecht is too small to establish a community center in every neighborhood.

121865.explanation: In the center, you can park for free for 2 hours, and that is sufficient.

121866.explanation: There is little space and already enough parking spaces in the center.

121868.explanation: Being able to openly be yourself is increasingly under pressure. D66 is committed to diversity on an ongoing basis.

121812.explanation: Each party appoints its own candidate who is competent to participate as an alderman. These can be men or women. For SGP Papendrecht, it is important to have a good team.

121813.explanation: The SGP finds it important that residents have a say in topics that concern them. We prefer to use other instruments than a referendum. A referendum does not fit within the current local system. Moreover, a referendum often presents a complex issue too simply.

121816.explanation: SGP Papendrecht is happy with hospitality businesses in Papendrecht. Much is going well. Still, we believe it is good to have equal agreements for all parties. Therefore, the government must record clear rules in the APV. SGP Papendrecht especially wants to keep nights and Sundays quiet.

121817.explanation: SGP Papendrecht wants to encourage residents and think along with them to generate sustainable energy together. The municipality should not financially support this, because there are mainly schemes from the national government. The municipality should primarily focus on saving energy use.

121819.explanation: SGP Papendrecht supports asylum reception that matches the scale and carrying capacity of Papendrecht. We understand the concerns and want to accommodate them by taking appropriate measures where necessary. However, the SGP also wants to be a reliable party that stands by agreements made.

121823.explanation: SGP Papendrecht believes it is not the municipality's task to organize a central fireworks show. However, the SGP wants to facilitate that residents or businesses can organize their own fireworks show. In our view, a fireworks show plays no role in reducing fireworks use.

121824.explanation: SGP Papendrecht believes that residents should clean up their own waste where possible. This also applies to bulky waste. If that is not possible, costs may be charged. After all, it costs the collection service money. By charging money, people are encouraged to clean up their own waste.

121825.explanation: SGP Papendrecht finds that more consideration should be given to residents near festivals and parties. Noise certainly plays an important role. We do not want to relax noise standards. And perhaps even prefer to make them stricter, as the statement suggests.

121826.explanation: SGP Papendrecht is committed to the accessibility of buildings for people with disabilities. When it concerns municipal buildings, we must of course pay for that ourselves. Entrepreneurs should arrange and pay for this themselves. However, the municipality should encourage them.

121828.explanation: SGP Papendrecht finds it important that there are sufficient local media. This includes the local (now regional) broadcaster. No extra funding is needed.

121832.explanation: SGP Papendrecht wants to preserve the historical elements in Papendrecht. There are not many. So we will not cut back on that.

121833.explanation: SGP Papendrecht believes the safety of Papendrecht residents is very important. BOAs contribute to this. For example, in reducing nuisance from fatbikes. Therefore, we believe more should be deployed.

121834.explanation: SGP Papendrecht finds safety important. Sometimes it may be necessary to temporarily deploy camera surveillance. The mayor can decide this independently. We want this to remain and therefore do not necessarily want more camera surveillance.

121836.explanation: SGP Papendrecht believes that BOAs in Papendrecht basically have sufficient resources. If BOAs get heavier responsibilities in exceptional cases, their equipment must be adapted accordingly so they can do their work safely.

121838.explanation: SGP Papendrecht wants Papendrecht housing seekers to have a chance to get a place in a home in Papendrecht. The SGP believes that not only other groups should get priority. Of course, we comply with laws and regulations, but we do not want Papendrecht residents to be skipped or get too little chance.

121841.explanation: SGP Papendrecht wants to use opportunities to build houses as much as possible. If offices are unnecessarily empty, they should certainly be converted into homes. Where necessary, the municipality must contribute to making this possible, even with money.

121843.explanation: SGP Papendrecht gladly helps people get benefits when needed. Conversely, we believe these people should be asked to contribute to society where possible. For example, through volunteer work. There is truly a great need for volunteers!

121844.explanation: SGP Papendrecht finds it important that everyone can continue to participate. The principle is that everyone participates and pays according to their means. Where necessary, we forgive municipal taxes. That is currently the case and as far as we are concerned, it should remain so.

121845.explanation: The SGP wants to prevent poverty and contribute where necessary to improving the situation. The municipality currently has a generous minimum policy. As far as the SGP is concerned, it should remain so. If the income limit rises further, working becomes less worthwhile. We also consider that important.

121848.explanation: SGP Papendrecht holds traffic safety in high regard and we find sufficient traffic flow important. Therefore, we want 30 km/h zones in neighborhoods. On more main roads, the speed remains 50 km/h. Additionally, the layout of main roads does not fit 30 km/h.

121850.explanation: SGP Papendrecht believes that a low-emission zone does not fit the scale of Papendrecht. Furthermore, there is too much influence from traffic flows outside Papendrecht via water and road to make this an effective measure.

121851.explanation: SGP Papendrecht finds it important that someone can choose a care provider who suits him or her. The SGP will continue to advocate for faith-based care.

121853.explanation: SGP Papendrecht finds that every woman who needs tampons and sanitary pads should be able to have them. For some residents, this costs too much. We see more in national measures to strengthen existential security or local social initiatives such as neighborhood cabinets.

121855.explanation: The SGP wants local charges to remain below the national average. The tourist tax is currently well below that, so it can be raised.

121862.explanation: SGP Papendrecht finds livable neighborhoods important. We believe much high-rise construction does not fit the scale and character of Papendrecht. Therefore, we want to be very cautious about tall residential buildings. We prefer lower apartment complexes instead.

121863.explanation: SGP Papendrecht believes the housing market requires strong measures. Partitioning or room rental fits within that. It is important, however, to prevent abuses.

121864.explanation: SGP Papendrecht wants to strengthen the neighborhoods. Livable neighborhoods also include neighborhood centers. This could be well done in or near primary schools.

121865.explanation: SGP Papendrecht is satisfied with the current parking rates in the center. The first 2 hours are free. We want to keep it that way.

121866.explanation: SGP Papendrecht does not have the impression that this is currently a problem. So let's focus on other matters!

121868.explanation: SGP Papendrecht finds it important that every resident can participate in the municipality. As far as we are concerned, this applies every day! In the SGP's conviction, campaigns are not helpful. They often lead to polarization. Including one leads to excluding another.

121812.explanation: We realistically choose a new council without imposed equality, focusing on quality, experience, and local involvement.

121813.explanation: We support more direct citizen participation and see referenda as a realistic means to achieve this.

121816.explanation: We believe entrepreneurs can realistically arrange their opening hours in consultation with the municipality.

121817.explanation: We realistically support citizen initiatives for sustainable energy, provided they align with the municipality and contracts are reviewed by the municipality for responsibility and affordability.

121819.explanation: We disagree with immediate closure: realistically, we must respect the Distribution Act and honor agreements, while applying maximum pressure on The Hague for fair distribution. Additionally, we commit to thoroughly monitoring the COA's operations.

121823.explanation: We are in favor of a joint fireworks display on the water with neighboring municipalities.

121824.explanation: We want an affordable bulky waste pickup service alongside the free Milleustraat, realistically with a small fee to prevent abuse and keep costs low for all residents.

121825.explanation: We oppose stricter noise standards for festivals and parties and realistically choose a balance between events, entrepreneurs, and livability through clear agreements.

121826.explanation: We actively engage in dialogue with entrepreneurs and aim to assist with national subsidies for accessibility (without additional municipal costs).

121828.explanation: We support the local broadcaster only if the current subsidy is demonstrably spent effectively; realistically, we opt for improvements through self-generated revenue models instead of extra tax money.

121832.explanation: We oppose cuts to monument care and realistically choose to preserve Papendrecht’s heritage as a core value of identity and livability.

121833.explanation: We want more enforcement officers (BOA’s) in Papendrecht, with a clear focus on safety and visible presence.

121834.explanation: We want to deploy enforcement officers earlier in Papendrecht, focusing clearly on safety and visible presence. This preserves human contact and prevents privacy violations.

121836.explanation: Enforcement officers equipped with batons provide realistic law enforcement and self-defense against aggression.

121838.explanation: We support priority for Papendrecht residents in social rental housing to prioritize local families and the community.

121841.explanation: We oppose subsidies for converting vacant offices but allow room for the market.

121843.explanation: We support a reciprocity requirement for welfare benefits: realistic work for the unemployed promotes personal responsibility and order in Papendrecht.

121844.explanation: We do not see tax forgiveness as ideal, but realistically apply tailored solutions, examining each case to understand why a resident can no longer pay taxes.

121845.explanation: Realistically, we choose tailored approaches to poverty reduction: in each case we look at how residents fell into poverty and what can help them recover.

121848.explanation: We oppose a general 30 km/h speed limit within the built-up area. Realistically, we keep 50 km/h for smooth traffic flow, with targeted measures at schools and better enforcement.

121850.explanation: We oppose an environmental zone in Papendrecht. Realistically, we will not ban vehicles from the center but maintain a sober policy for free passage.

121851.explanation: In our view, it is not realistic to provide care for every belief system within Papendrecht. This can be better achieved in cooperation with the region. Through citizen participation, we can properly assess local needs.

121853.explanation: We oppose free menstrual products provided by the municipality; realistically, the food bank can play a role for vulnerable individuals through their packages.

121855.explanation: We keep Papendrecht attractive without additional levies.

121862.explanation: We are realistic by listening to Papendrecht’s needs through citizen participation.

121863.explanation: We oppose subdividing properties into multiple small residences; this protects Papendrecht neighborhoods from overcrowding.

121864.explanation: The municipality is too small to have a neighborhood center in every district; a central neighborhood center could be established where a special neighborhood program runs at certain times: town hall-library-community center?

121865.explanation: We favor cheaper paid parking to make Papendrecht more attractive to shoppers and entrepreneurs.

121866.explanation: We support additional parking spaces in the center and want to realistically assess demand.

121868.explanation: We oppose an annual diversity campaign, thereby not wasting tax money on symbolism, but focusing on unity and order.

121812.explanation: Quality comes before gender; for 50PLUS, men and women are equal. However, we do strive for balance.

121813.explanation: Residents must be able to have a say in everything. A referendum is a good tool for this.

121816.explanation: Provided that the rules of the General Local Regulation (APV) are observed, including those regarding possible nuisance.

121817.explanation: Exemption from energy tax is already possible under the SCE scheme.

121819.explanation: The location at Weteringsingel, in the middle of the De Meent shopping center, is a prime example of a wrong location choice. Convert it into starter homes and realize shelter elsewhere.

121823.explanation: Provided collectively with neighboring municipalities, from a cost-sharing perspective.

121824.explanation: This prevents illegal dumping at waste containers.

121825.explanation: Noise nuisance must be more controlled and existing laws must be enforced.

121826.explanation: For new construction or renovations, the owner must pay. For older buildings, the municipality can contribute, in the interest of the population.

121828.explanation: The broadcaster is already entitled to financial support. Regional cooperation works cost-effectively.

121832.explanation: There are few monuments in Papendrecht and private initiatives could possibly do more for their preservation.

121833.explanation: Police (community officers) and enforcement officers need to be more visible on the streets. More effort means more personnel.

121834.explanation: Provided it is permanent, at designated locations, with a clear and specific purpose. Mobile installations only work temporarily, so ultimately they do not.

121836.explanation: Enforcement officers must be able to protect themselves and others against the troublemakers who threaten them. A baton is a light defensive weapon but effective.

121838.explanation: Social rental homes exist for a reason. Every Papendrecht resident has the right to a good and affordable roof over their head. If they cannot achieve this themselves, our community should lend a helping hand to keep them part of our Papendrecht community.

121841.explanation: To make the renovation possible, provided it is in the form of a loan that must be repaid.

121843.explanation: Social assistance should basically and as much as possible be temporary. Offering paid work is the appropriate way to avoid the need for social assistance. Through reintegration, both the individual and the community are helped. We fundamentally reject forced volunteer work as undesirable.

121844.explanation: Necessary help to the local most disadvantaged takes many forms. Cutting back on this is not what 50PLUS stands for.

121845.explanation: Inflation affects everyone, but the most at the bottom, in cases of poverty. This must continue to be compensated.

121848.explanation: Not 30 km/h as a general measure for the entire municipality. The current 50 km/h is suitable in almost all cases. Customized solutions in special situations are always possible.

121850.explanation: The municipality has a duty of care. In this context, the duty to ensure as healthy a living environment as possible comes first.

121851.explanation: Care must be arranged equally for everyone, especially through the collective basic insurance. Those who want more or different coverage can take out supplementary insurance. It should not be at the expense of everyone.

121853.explanation: And similarly contraceptives, with corresponding education. Personal and social hygiene go hand in hand.

121855.explanation: It currently yields relatively little, because the current amount is lower than average. Besides, Papendrecht has little tourism.

121862.explanation: In well-chosen locations, apartment buildings are an example of modern, responsible policy, provided they are not too high and are aesthetically designed. They also generate the most revenue per square meter of sold land for the municipality.

121863.explanation: Exploitation through excessively high rents and deterioration driven by a cost-saving and overcrowding profit model is looming. The additional control and enforcement burden will also become too heavy for the municipal apparatus.

121864.explanation: Community centers promote social cohesion in society. Everyone benefits from them.

121865.explanation: 2 hours of free parking in two parking garages is sufficient in our village. The option of long (almost) free parking on the street leads to undesirably higher parking pressure.

121866.explanation: There is little space for this. The current situation is not exceptional and generally still adequate, depending on the neighborhood and its size.

121868.explanation: It is not a task of the municipality. Every minority and interest group can freely organize in our country; also in our municipality. Shoemaker, stick to your last.

121812.explanation: Leaders must be chosen based on quality, experience, and commitment to residents, not gender. Equal opportunities are important, but mandatory quotas do not fit a free and democratic governing choice.

121813.explanation: Residents should have more direct influence on important decisions that affect their living environment. A referendum strengthens democracy and ensures that the voice of residents is heard, not just that of the administration.

121816.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be bothered as little as possible by the municipality. Cafés and restaurants themselves know best when they can and want to be open. Fewer rules mean more freedom for entrepreneurs and a lively village.

121817.explanation: The PVV is against extra subsidies for sustainable projects. Energy initiatives should be able to operate without municipal support. The municipality should not use taxpayers’ money for climate policies that lead to higher burdens for residents.

121819.explanation: Asylum reception puts pressure on housing, facilities, and safety. The municipality must stand up for its own residents. Asylum reception is a national task and should not be passed on to municipalities.

121823.explanation: A fireworks show can contribute to safety and social cohesion, but it is not a core task of the municipality. If there is support and budget, it can be done, but it should not become an obligation or come at the expense of more important tasks.

121824.explanation: Free bulky waste collection prevents illegal dumping and extra litter on the streets. Residents already pay enough municipal taxes. The municipality must ensure a clean living environment without extra costs for its residents.

121825.explanation: Festivals and parties create coziness and connection in the village. Existing noise standards already provide protection against nuisance. Stricter rules are unnecessary and limit events and clubs too much.

121826.explanation: Accessibility is important, but entrepreneurs should not become dependent on municipal subsidies. The municipality can stimulate and think along, but financial aid must be voluntary and targeted, not automatic or mandatory.

121828.explanation: Local media are important for information provision, but extra subsidy should not be automatic. The municipality can provide support if it demonstrably contributes to local engagement but must handle community funds carefully.

121832.explanation: The preservation of monuments is important, but only when the financial space allows it. Municipal funds should primarily be used for essential tasks such as safety, care, and livability.

121833.explanation: More visible enforcement increases safety and livability in Papendrecht. Additional enforcement officers ensure faster response to nuisance and strengthen residents’ sense of security, as a complement to the police.

121834.explanation: Camera surveillance helps prevent and detect crime and increases residents' sense of security. Extra surveillance in risk areas is an effective means to address nuisance and offenses.

121836.explanation: Enforcement officers increasingly face aggression and violence. A baton can contribute to their own safety and authority on the street. Provided they are well trained and responsibly used, this is an appropriate means to perform their work safely.

121838.explanation: Residents who live in Papendrecht or have ties to it should have priority for social rental housing. This keeps housing affordable for local residents and prevents scarce housing being taken by people from outside the municipality.

121841.explanation: Converting vacant offices can contribute to an increase in the number of homes, but subsidies should not be automatic. The municipality can think along and facilitate, but market parties remain primarily responsible themselves for profitable redevelopment.

121843.explanation: Those receiving social assistance may give something back in return. Socially meaningful work increases involvement, helps people stay active, and increases the chance of transitioning to paid work, with continued guidance and customized support.

121844.explanation: Residents with a low income often already have financial difficulties. Exemption from municipal taxes prevents additional debts and financial problems. The municipality must protect vulnerable residents and not put further pressure on them.

121845.explanation: The municipality must keep poverty policy targeted and affordable. Raising income limits makes programs more expensive and less targeted. Help should be available for those who really need it, not through increasingly broad general arrangements.

121848.explanation: A general reduction to 30 km/h is unnecessary and hinders traffic flow. At dangerous or busy places, 30 km/h may be appropriate, but on main roads 50 km/h should remain possible for accessibility and traffic.

121850.explanation: An environmental zone excludes residents and entrepreneurs who depend on their vehicle. It is an expensive and unfair measure that has little effect on air quality. The municipality should focus on feasible solutions without banning people.

121851.explanation: Healthcare must be equal, accessible, and affordable for everyone. It is not always feasible to organize care based on religion or culture. The quality of care and availability of staff must always come first.

121853.explanation: No one should be limited in meeting basic daily needs due to lack of money. Free tampons and sanitary pads help women and girls with low income and prevent health problems and shame. The municipality can provide practical support in this way.

121855.explanation: Raising the tourist tax makes the municipality less attractive for visitors and entrepreneurs. Additional levies discourage visitors and yield little. The municipality should focus on hospitality and the local economy, not on higher charges.

121862.explanation: Housing construction is necessary, but tall apartment buildings do not fit everywhere in Papendrecht. The municipality should assess each location's spatial and social desirability, considering liveability, village character, and support among residents.

121863.explanation: Room rental can sometimes help with housing shortages but must not lead to nuisances or exploitation. Rent prices must remain reasonable and affordable. The municipality should only permit this where it fits and can be properly enforced.

121864.explanation: Community centers can contribute to social interaction and cohesion, but not every neighborhood has the same need. The municipality should identify where a community center truly adds value and better utilize existing facilities instead of building new ones everywhere.

121865.explanation: Cheap parking keeps the center attractive for visitors and local entrepreneurs. Even with 2 hours of free parking, lowering rates can contribute to more foot traffic and a lively shopping area. The municipality should remove barriers.

121866.explanation: Although there is already 2 hours of free parking, cheaper paid parking afterward can keep visitors in the center longer. This supports local entrepreneurs and creates more vibrancy without limiting accessibility.

121868.explanation: The municipality should focus on its core tasks. Mandatory annual campaigns cost money and have limited effect. Equal treatment is already legally established; living together is done in practice, not through ever new campaigns.

statements.title: The municipality must organize an annual diversity campaign.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet elk jaar een campagne voor

titleParts.glossary: Differences between people, for example regarding cultural background or sexual orientation.

statements.title: The residents of Papendrecht should be able to voice their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Papendrecht moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Paid parking in the center should be made cheaper.
The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
There should be more camera surveillance in Papendrecht.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The BOAs who enforce safety in Papendrecht should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Papendrecht controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Papendrecht should introduce an environmental zone to keep polluting vehicles out of the center.

titleParts.glossary: An area where very polluting cars are not allowed to drive.

titleParts.text: invoeren om vervuilende voertuigen uit het centrum te weren.

statements.title: More high-rise apartment buildings may be allowed in Papendrecht.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: There should be more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) in Papendrecht who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.
